stories.headline: US-China tensions rise over technology and geopolitical disputes
stories.summary: The Trump administration plans to increase enforcement actions against Chinese companies it says are improperly accessing or using American artificial intelligence models without authorization. China has denied the accusations of technology theft, framing them as baseless while developing its own competing AI systems.

angles.headline: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' U.S. AI models
angles.summary: The Trump administration plans to increase enforcement actions against Chinese companies it says are improperly accessing or using American artificial intelligence models without authorization. China has denied the accusations of technology theft, framing them as baseless while developing its own competing AI systems.

angles.headline: In lead-up to Trump’s Beijing visit, China announces 2 pandas are headed to US zoo
angles.summary: China announces it will send two giant pandas to the Atlanta Zoo, a gesture that precedes President Trump's scheduled visit to Beijing. Panda diplomacy has long served as a tool for strengthening bilateral relations between the two countries.

angles.headline: China bans dual-use items exports to seven European entities over Taiwan arms sales
angles.summary: China has restricted exports of dual-use materials and technology to seven European entities in response to their involvement in arms sales to Taiwan. The move reflects Beijing's effort to impose costs on countries it views as supporting Taiwan's military capabilities.

stories.headline: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.summary: A couple learned their home in Lebanon was destroyed by Israeli forces only after seeing satellite imagery, highlighting the scale of displacement across the country. The discovery underscores ongoing tensions in southern Lebanon, where military operations have continued despite ceasefire agreements.

stories.headline: US soldier charged with using Polymarket to bet on Nicolas Maduro abduction
stories.summary: A U.S. soldier faces charges for allegedly using classified information about military operations targeting Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro to place bets on a cryptocurrency prediction market. The soldier reportedly profited substantially from wagering on Maduro's capture or removal through Polymarket, a decentralized betting platform.

stories.headline: India denounces ‘hellhole’ remark shared by Trump
stories.summary: India's government has formally objected to a social media post by Donald Trump that included characterizations of India as a "hellhole," which officials described as inappropriate and in poor taste. The statement reflects tensions over Trump's amplification of commentary critical of Indian immigrants.

stories.headline: China’s DeepSeek unveils latest models a year after upending global tech
stories.summary: DeepSeek has released a new generation of AI models one year after its previous releases disrupted global tech markets by offering capable alternatives at lower costs. The launch demonstrates China's continued advancement in open-source artificial intelligence development and signals ongoing competition in a field previously dominated by Western companies.

stories.headline: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: An international court has confirmed charges against former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte for crimes against humanity, moving his case toward trial proceedings. The decision stems from his government's war on drugs campaign, which resulted in thousands of deaths during his 2016-2022 presidency.

stories.headline: EU approves a $106 billion loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
stories.summary: The European Union has approved a $106 billion loan package for Ukraine after Hungary withdrew its opposition, resolving a months-long blockade within the bloc. The funding will support Ukraine's economic stability and reconstruction efforts as the country continues its conflict with Russia.

stories.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: A senior military official suspected of orchestrating the 2013 Tadamon massacre, which killed over 200 people during Syria's civil war, has been arrested in Damascus. The arrest marks a rare accountability measure for a documented atrocity committed more than a decade ago.

stories.headline: Ice block stalls hundreds of Everest climbers at base camp
stories.summary: A large ice block on Mount Everest's standard climbing route has halted progress for hundreds of climbers attempting the spring expedition season. The obstruction delays the installation of fixed ropes and ladders needed to establish safe passage through the mountain's highest sections.

stories.headline: Ukrainian married couple aged 75 killed in Russian attack on Odesa
stories.summary: A Russian strike on the Ukrainian port city of Odesa has killed a 75-year-old married couple, along with damage to residential areas and critical infrastructure including hospitals and schools. The attack also wounded at least 15 people and damaged port facilities in the overnight bombardment.
